dataset: NOAA OI SST v2 Long-Term Mean

pi_items.source: File path in setup_task.sh and global download script
Extracted from NOAA sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc dataset via xarray
Output paths defined in setup_task.sh and verifier.py
Mandate text in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh checks

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes real NOAA Optimum Interpolation (OI) SST V2 Long-Term Mean data (sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc), verifying that the data is not synthetic. The required variable is 'sst' and the outputs are verified to exactly match the target paths in /home/ga/Documents/ASWOceanography. Independent data analysis confirms that the true maximum thermal amplitude (August vs. February) in the Northern Hemisphere exceeds 26°C (e.g., Caspian Sea at 26.36°C, Bohai/Yellow Sea at 25.12°C, and NW Atlantic at 24.7°C). However, the verifier explicitly limits the acceptable max amplitude range to between 16.0 and 24.0, which means an agent reporting the mathematically precise peak >24.0 would incorrectly fail the amplitude check.
